message:
test(frankenpandas): add Categorical Analysis integration test (br-q0foo / fd90.159)

fd90.159: completes the README → integration-test coverage pattern
(fd90.146 / fd90.156 / fd90.157 / fd90.158). The README's 'Categorical
Analysis' example (lines 1350-1379) wasn't exercised by any
integration test. CategoricalAccessor + CategoricalMetadata are
top-level re-exports (lib.rs:37) but not in the prelude;
Series::from_categorical is reachable via the in-prelude Series type.

Fix: add 'readme_categorical_analysis_compiles_and_runs' which mirrors
the README example. Constructs a 4-element categorical Series ['good',
'poor', 'excellent', 'good'] and verifies:
  - Series::from_categorical(name, Vec<Scalar>, ordered: bool)
  - .cat() returning Option<CategoricalAccessor>
  - cat.categories() — 3 unique values in first-seen order
  - cat.codes()?.values() — [0, 1, 2, 0] (good repeats at end)
  - cat.rename_categories(vec!["Good", "Poor", "Excellent".into()...])?
  - renamed.cat().unwrap().to_values()? — round-trip back to a
    Series of labels (length preserved at 4)

After this, the four major Recipes sections of README (Financial Data
Pipeline, Merge-Asof, Categorical Analysis, MultiIndex Operations)
all have integration-test coverage, plus Quick Example, Quick Start,
Merge: Advanced Options, and Expression-Driven Analysis.

Verified:
- cargo test -p frankenpandas — 7 lib + 6 integration + 2 doctests
  all pass

Pure additive test. No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-q0foo","title":"fd90.159: add Categorical Analysis integration test (Series::from_categorical/.cat()/rename_categories)","description":"fd90.74-158 follow-on. Last unpacked README recipe. The 'Categorical Analysis' example (lines 1350-1379) exercises Series::from_categorical + .cat() + categories() + codes() + rename_categories + to_values — none of which are exercised by any integration test today. CategoricalAccessor + CategoricalMetadata are top-level re-exports (lib.rs:37) but not in the prelude; Series::from_categorical is reachable via the in-prelude Series type.

Fix: add 'readme_categorical_analysis_compiles_and_runs' which mirrors the README example. Verifies:
  - Series::from_categorical(name, Vec<Scalar>, ordered: bool)
  - ratings.cat().unwrap() returning CategoricalAccessor
  - cat.categories() returning &[Scalar]
  - cat.codes()?.values() returning &[Scalar] of Int64 codes
  - cat.rename_categories(Vec<Scalar>) returning Result<Series>
  - renamed.cat().unwrap().to_values()? returning Result<Series>

Verify with cargo test -p frankenpandas.  {"id":"frankenpandas-q4t","title":"Add non-CSV IO packet conformance coverage","description":"Testing-conformance audit found fp-conformance has CSV packet fixtures and fuzz/round-trip helpers for JSON/JSONL/Excel/Parquet/Feather, but the packet oracle surface does not cover pandas IO parity for non-CSV formats. Add oracle-backed packet coverage for JSON orient modes and JSONL records, then add deterministic round-trip parity packets for Excel, Parquet, Feather, IPC stream, and SQL read/write where pandas oracle comparison is feasible. Include malformed-input error fixtures and schema/order/null preservation checks. Evidence: README advertises seven IO formats; fp-conformance FixtureOperation exposes CsvRoundTrip only; lib.rs has helper/fuzz routines for the other formats but no packet variants.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:26:35.913897138Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:28:20.561118598Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:28:20.559404657Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","io"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-q554z","title":"fd90.126: README NanOps block uses bare nan* calls but fp_types::nan* not re-exported from frankenpandas","description":"fd90.74-125 README audit. NanOps code block at lines 784-810 shows bare calls:

  nansum(&values)
  nanmean(&values)
  nancount(&values)
  ... (23 nan* functions in total)

But crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs:20 only re-exports a subset of fp_types functions (cast_scalar, common_dtype, infer_dtype, isna, isnull, notna, notnull). None of the 23 nan* functions are re-exported, so they're not reachable from 'use frankenpandas::prelude::*' (or even 'use frankenpandas::*').

A user copy-pasting hits 23 E0425 'cannot find function' errors.

The block has no 'use fp_types::*' or explicit qualification, so it reads as if the prelude exposes these functions. fd90.121 + fd90.125 set the precedent that the prelude should match the README's documented surface.

Fix: re-export all 23 nan* functions at the frankenpandas crate top-level (alongside cast_scalar, common_dtype etc.) AND add them to the prelude. The expansion list:
  Reductions (19): nansum, nanmean, nancount, nanmin, nanmax, nanmedian,
                   nanvar, nanstd, nansem, nanprod, nanptp, nanskew,
                   nankurt, nanquantile, nanargmax, nanargmin, nannunique,
                   nanany, nanall
  Cumulative (4): nancumsum, nancumprod, nancummax, nancummin

Verify with cargo build -p frankenpandas + cargo test -p frankenpandas --lib. Code change to fp-frankenpandas; pure re-export expansion.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:49:28.002066789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:51:25.211525766Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:51:25.208827175Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
